entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop [Pennisetum glaucum (L.) R. Br.] used for food and fodder

entities.name: ARID REGIONS
entities.description: Dry environmental areas where pearl millet is typically cultivated

entities.name: FOODGRAIN
entities.description: The grain part of the pearl millet crop used for human consumption

entities.name: DRY FODDER
entities.description: Dried plant material from pearl millet used as animal feed

entities.name: RUMINANT LIVESTOCK
entities.description: Animals that feed on dry fodder from pearl millet

entities.name: FEEDING VALUE
entities.description: The nutritional worth of crop residues for livestock

entities.name: CROP RESIDUES
entities.description: The remaining plant parts, such as stover and chaff, after grain harvest

entities.name: STOVER
entities.description: The stalks and leaves of the pearl millet plant

entities.name: CHAFF
entities.description: The husks of the pearl millet grain

entities.name: ICMV 221
entities.description: A widely-adapted variety of pearl millet used as a base population for selection

entities.name: RECURRENT SELECTION
entities.description: A breeding method involving repeated cycles of selection to improve traits

entities.name: FULL-SIB PROGENY
entities.description: Offspring from the same parents used in the selection process

entities.name: STOVER NUTRITIONAL QUALITY
entities.description: The specific nutritional attributes of stover targeted for improvement

entities.name: OPEN-POLLINATED CULTIVARS
entities.description: Plant varieties pollinated by open means, often accepted by farmers
